description:
Trigger: HSM transiently disconnected (CKR_DEVICE_REMOVED, CKR_TOKEN_NOT_PRESENT, CKR_DEVICE_ERROR), network HSM unreachable, PKCS#11 daemon restarted, USB token unplugged-replugged, slot temporarily unavailable.

Bad behavior: every cryptoki error is wrapped as SecretError::Backend { backend: "pkcs11", source: e.into() } at lines 91, 95, 102, 109, 141, 168, 207, 215, 231, 239, 266, 330, 402, 416, 491, 502, 539. secretx-core docs (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:976-1012) explicitly distinguish Backend (permanent, do not retry) from Unavailable (transient, retry with backoff). Operator-facing tooling that auto-retries on Unavailable will abort instead, treating a temporarily disconnected HSM as a permanent failure.

Fix: classify cryptoki errors. CKR_DEVICE_REMOVED, CKR_TOKEN_NOT_PRESENT, CKR_DEVICE_ERROR, CKR_FUNCTION_FAILED-with-recoverable-rv, network errors from PKCS#11-over-network proxies, and session-not-open errors should map to SecretError::Unavailable. Authentication failures (CKR_PIN_INCORRECT, CKR_USER_NOT_LOGGED_IN), object-not-found, and malformed-template errors stay as Backend.
title: Transient PKCS#11 errors mapped to permanent SecretError::Backend instead of Unavailable
